content_type: memorial / Wall of Honor tribute card
description: A designed 'Wall of Honor' memorial tribute graphic in landscape (1200x675) format honoring a fallen legacy officer. The left portion shows white serif text 'Wall of Honor' over a dark, moody slate-blue textured background, accompanied by a stylized ICE memorial badge with a blue mourning ribbon (badge reads 'ICE / U.S.'). Below in serif type: 'Deportation Officer / Frank E. Chaffin', a thin gold horizontal rule, 'End of Watch' (with the 'of' in italic), another gold rule, and the date 'Wednesday, January 28, 1948'. The center holds a large square inset of a grainy black-and-white archival portrait photograph of an older man (balding, in a suit and tie), consistent with a 1940s-era photo. The right side features a warm photograph of red roses in front of a softly blurred American flag, evoking remembrance and patriotism. There are no suspects, no enforcement scenes, and no aggressive messaging. Technique: solemn, patriotic commemoration (flag, roses, mourning-ribbon badge, refined serif typography) used to build the agency's institutional heritage and valorize officer sacrifice; the tweet notes Chaffin died in a 1948 plane crash while deporting aliens. Target audience: the public and agency personnel; intent is reverence, legacy-building, and legitimacy.
notable_text: Wall of Honor. Deportation Officer Frank E. Chaffin. End of Watch. Wednesday, January 28, 1948.
